mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation trend combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate sustains real-rate compression and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-real-rate periods do not show higher 20d SPX returns than baseline, or if the sign of the return effect is negative in more than 55% of out-of-sample triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE signals improving labor-market income and consumption expectations, which lifts earnings optimism and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE declines meeting the trigger are followed by negative 60d SPX returns in at least 50% of instances or if the average 60d return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to its recent trend indicates strengthening nominal demand and retail revenue momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month moving average rises above its prior 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum triggers are not associated with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and reduced recession risk, which supports cyclical equity performance and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.4% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO expansion triggers fail to improve 20d SPX returns versus unconditional returns, or if the average return effect turns negative after accounting for revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above trend reflect easing credit conditions and stronger construction demand, which leads equity investors to price better near-term growth and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts-above-trend periods do not produce higher 60d SPX returns than baseline, or if the sign is negative in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated and CPIAUCSL inflation is still falling, policy is restrictive relative to realized price dynamics, so discount rates dominate and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation trend is negative
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-policy/declining-inflation triggers are not followed by lower-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the negative sign does not persist across at least two rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE signals broad labor-market healing, which boosts income expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined payroll gains and unemployment declines do not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60d SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle slowdown, weak GDPC1 growth together with soft RSAFS signals demand deceleration and margin pressure, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth falls below 1.5% and RSAFS monthly growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if low-growth/weak-sales episodes do not lead to negative 20d SPX returns, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.
